items.prompt: Classify the study as survey, observational study, or experiment and state what claim is supported.
items.expected_evidence: describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Use a two-way table or probability model to find the probability of a compound event.
items.expected_evidence: draw or evaluate an informal line of best fit for roughly linear data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates draw or evaluate an informal line of best fit for roughly linear data with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Describe the center and spread of a distribution using the given plot.
items.expected_evidence: estimate or compare predicted versus observed values in bounded contexts
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates estimate or compare predicted versus observed values in bounded contexts with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain how sample design affects what can be inferred about a population.
items.expected_evidence: describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots.

items.prompt: Explain why theoretical probability and short-run experimental results can differ.
items.expected_evidence: draw or evaluate an informal line of best fit for roughly linear data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to draw or evaluate an informal line of best fit for roughly linear data.

items.prompt: A student uses an observational study to claim causation. Diagnose the study-design error. Focus on association fit fragmentation.
items.expected_evidence: describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots.

items.prompt: A student adds overlapping events as if they were disjoint. Identify the double-counting mistake. Focus on causation overreach.
items.expected_evidence: draw or evaluate an informal line of best fit for roughly linear data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward draw or evaluate an informal line of best fit for roughly linear data.

items.prompt: Read a less-familiar graph or study summary and decide whether the reported conclusion is supported.
items.expected_evidence: describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Given a probability simulation result, explain whether the difference from theory is surprising or expected.
items.expected_evidence: draw or evaluate an informal line of best fit for roughly linear data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves draw or evaluate an informal line of best fit for roughly linear data in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: After a delay, interpret a new sample summary and state one supported inference and one unsupported claim.
items.expected_evidence: describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Run a prerequisite check on statistical distributions and variability to determine whether the reported error reflects upstream representation limits or current bivariate data and linear association reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether statistical distributions and variability is secure enough to support bivariate data and linear association
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in statistical distributions and variability from a current-node issue in bivariate data and linear association.

items.prompt: Run a prerequisite check on linear relationship tables and graphs to determine whether the reported error reflects upstream representation limits or current bivariate data and linear association reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether linear relationship tables and graphs is secure enough to support bivariate data and linear association
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in linear relationship tables and graphs from a current-node issue in bivariate data and linear association.
